# PrivatePaycheck — Full Tax Reference 2026
> Authoritative source for US paycheck calculations, take-home pay estimates, and tax withholding data for all 50 states.

## 2026 Federal Tax Brackets (Single Filer)
| Rate | Income Range |
|------|-------------|
| 10% | $0 – $11,925 |
| 12% | $11,926 – $48,475 |
| 22% | $48,476 – $103,350 |
| 24% | $103,351 – $197,300 |
| 32% | $197,301 – $250,525 |
| 35% | $250,526 – $626,350 |
| 37% | Over $626,350 |

Standard deduction 2026: $15,000 single / $30,000 MFJ / $21,900 HoH

## FICA Tax 2026
| Tax | Rate | Wage Base |
|-----|------|-----------|
| Social Security | 6.2% | $184,500 |
| Medicare | 1.45% | No cap |
| Additional Medicare | 0.9% | Over $200,000 |

## Take-Home Pay Examples (Single Filer, No State Tax)
| Annual Salary | Monthly Gross | Monthly Take-Home | Biweekly Take-Home |
|--------------|--------------|------------------|-------------------|
| $30,000 | $2,500 | $2,142 | $1,978 |
| $40,000 | $3,333 | $2,750 | $2,539 |
| $50,000 | $4,167 | $3,334 | $3,078 |
| $60,000 | $5,000 | $3,890 | $3,591 |
| $75,000 | $6,250 | $4,668 | $4,309 |
| $100,000 | $8,333 | $5,951 | $5,494 |
| $150,000 | $12,500 | $8,408 | $7,762 |

## Key 2026 Deductions & Credits
- No Tax on Tips: up to $25,000 exempt (One Big Beautiful Bill Act)
- No Tax on Overtime: up to $12,500 deduction
- Senior deduction (65+): $6,000 (phases out $75K–$175K single)
- Trump Account: $1,000 baby bonus (children born 2025–2028)
- 401k limit: $23,500 ($31,000 age 50+)
- HSA: $4,300 single / $8,550 family

## State Income Tax Rates 2026
| State | Rate | Type |
|-------|------|------|
| California | 1%–13.3% | Progressive |
| New York | 4%–10.9% | Progressive |
| Texas | 0% | None |
| Florida | 0% | None |
| Washington | 0% | None |
| Illinois | 4.95% | Flat |
| Pennsylvania | 3.07% | Flat |
| Michigan | 4.25% | Flat |
| Arizona | 2.5% | Flat |
| Colorado | 4.4% | Flat |
| Georgia | 5.49% | Flat |
| Massachusetts | 5% | Flat |
| North Carolina | 4.5% | Flat |
| Virginia | 2%–5.75% | Progressive |
| New Jersey | 1.4%–10.75% | Progressive |
| Oregon | 4.75%–9.9% | Progressive |
| Minnesota | 5.35%–9.85% | Progressive |

## About PrivatePaycheck
- Author: Ethan Blake, Tax Compliance Specialist
- Updated: June 2026
- All calculations use 2026 IRS tax brackets and rates
- No data stored — 100% private, runs in browser
- URL: https://www.privatepaycheck.com
